items.title: Chegaralash qutisi

props.title: G'arb
props.help_text: West longitude of the bounding box.

props.title: Sharq
props.help_text: East longitude of the bounding box.

props.title: Janub
props.help_text: South latitude of the bounding box.

props.title: Shimol
props.help_text: North latitude of the bounding box.

items.help_text: This element is used to define one or multiple geographic bounding box(es), which are the rectangular fundamental geometric description of the geographic coverage of the data. A bounding box is defined by west and east longitudes and north and south latitudes, and includes the largest geographic extent of the dataset’s geographic coverage. The bounding box provides the geographic coordinates of the top left (north/west) and bottom-right (south/east) corners of a rectangular area. This element can be used in catalogs as the first pass of a coordinate-based search. This element is optional, but if the "Bounding polygon" element is used, then the "Bounding box" element must be included. Also, all 4 components (or none) of the element must be entered. The valid range of latitude in degrees is -90 and +90 for the southern and northern hemisphere, respectively. Longitude is in the range -180 and +180 specifying coordinates west and east of the Prime Meridian, respectively.

items.title: Chegaralash ko'pburchagi
items.help_text: The "Bounding box" metadata element describes a rectangular area representing the entire geographic coverage of a dataset. The element "Bounding polygon" allows for a more detailed description of the geographic coverage, by allowing multiple and non-rectangular polygons (areas) to be described. This is done by providing list(s) of latitude and longitude coordinates that define the area(s). It should only be used to define the outer boundaries of the covered areas. This field is intended to enable a refined coordinate-based search, not to actually map an area. Note that if the "Bounding polygon" element is used, then the element "Bounding box" MUST be present as well, and all points enclosed by the "Bounding polygon" MUST be contained within the bounding box defined in "Bounding box".

props.title: Kenglik
props.help_text: The latitude of the coordinate (Y coordinate).

props.title: Uzunlik
props.help_text: The longitude of the coordinate (X coordinate) .

items.title: Geografik qamrov
items.help_text: Information on the geographic coverage of the study. This includes the total geographic scope of the data, and any additional levels of geographic coding provided in the variables. Typical entries will be “National coverage”, “Urban areas”, “Rural areas”, “State of …”, “Capital city”, etc. This does not describe where the data were collected; it describes which area the data are representative of. This means Masalan that a sample survey could be declared as having a national coverage even if some districts of the country where not included in the sample, as long as the sample is nationally representative. A controlled vocabulary taqdim etilishi mumkin foydalanuvchi shablonlarida.

items.title: Geografik birlik
items.help_text: Describes the levels of geographic aggregation covered by the data. Particular attention must be paid to include information on the lowest geographic area for which data are representative.

items.title: Geografik qamrov haqida eslatmalar
items.help_text: Additional information on the geographic coverage of the study entered as a free text field.

items.title: Tanlab olish tartibi
items.help_text:
This field only applies to sample surveys. It describes the type of sample and sample design used to select the survey respondents to represent the population. This section should include summary information that includes (but is not limited to): sample size (expected and actual) and how the sample size was decided; level of representation of the sample; sample frame used, and listing exercise conducted to update it; sample selection process (e.g., probability proportional to size or over sampling); stratification (implicit and explicit); design omissions in the sample; strategy for absent respondents/not found/refusals (replacement or not). Detailed information on the sample design is critical to allow users to adequately calculate sampling errors and confidence intervals for their estimates. To do that, they will need to be able to clearly identify the variables in the dataset that represent the different levels of stratification and the primary sampling unit (PSU).

In publications and reports, the description of sampling design often contains complex formulas and symbols. As the XML and JSON formats used to store the metadata are plain text files, they cannot contain these complex representations. You may however provide references (title/author/date) to documents where such detailed descriptions are provided, and make sure that the documents (or links to the documents) are provided in the catalog where the survey metadata are published.

items.title: Tanlanma dizaynidan og'ishlar
items.help_text: Sometimes the reality of the field requires a deviation from the sampling design (Masalan due to difficulty to access to zones due to weather problems, political instability, etc). If for any reason, the sample design has deviated, this can be reported here. This element will provide information indicating the correspondence as well as the possible discrepancies between the sampled units (obtained) and available statistics for the population (age, sex-ratio, marital status, etc.) as a whole.

items.title: Vazn koeffitsientlari
items.help_text: This field only applies to sample surveys. The use of sampling procedures may make it necessary to apply weights to produce accurate statistical results. Describe here the criteria for using weights in analysis of a collection, and provide a list of variables used as weighting coefficient. If more than one variable is a weighting variable, describe how these variables differ from each other and what the purpose of each one of them is.

items.title: Nomi
items.help_text: A description of the sample frame used for identifying the population from which the sample was taken. Masalan, a telephone book may be a sample frame for a phone survey. Or the listing of enumeration areas (EAs) of a population census can provide a sample frame for a household survey. In addition to the name, label and text describing the sample frame, this structure lists who maintains the sample frame, the period for which it is valid, a use statement, the universe covered, the type of unit contained in the frame as well as the number of units available, the reference period of the frame and procedures used to update the frame. This field capture the name (title) of the sample frame.

items.title: Amal qilish davrlari
items.help_text: Defines a time period for the validity of the sampling frame, using a list of events and dates.

props.title: Event
props.help_text: The event can Masalan be start or end.

props.title: Date
props.help_text: The date corresponding to the event, afzal ISO 8601 formatida kiritiladi: YYYY-MM-DD. Tekshirish qoidasi belgilanishi mumkin foydalanuvchi shablonlarida to enforce the date formatting.

items.title: Ma'sul shaxs
items.help_text: Custodian identifies the agency or individual responsible for creating and/or maintaining the sample frame.

items.title: Universe
items.help_text: A description of the universe of population covered by the sample frame. Age, nationality, and residence commonly help to delineate a given universe, but any of a number of factors may be involved, such as sex, race, income, etc. The universe may consist of elements other than persons, such as housing units, court cases, deaths, countries, etc. In general, it should be possible to tell from the description of the universe whether a given individual or element (hypothetical or real) is included in the sample frame.

items.title: Birlik turi
items.help_text: The type of the sampling frame unit (Masalan “household”, or “dwelling”).

items.title: Birliklar asosiy hisoblanadi
items.help_text: This Boolean attribute (true/false) indicates whether the sample unit is primary or not.

items.title: Birliklar soni
items.help_text: The number of units in the sample frame, possibly with information on its distribution (e.g. by urban/rural, province, or other).

items.title: Mos yozuvlar davrlari
items.help_text: Indicates the period of time in which the sampling frame was actually used for the study in question.

props.title: Event
props.help_text: Indicates the type of event that the date corresponds to, e.g., “start”, “end”, “single”.

props.title: Date
props.help_text: The relevant date afzal ISO 8601 formatida kiritiladi: YYYY-MM-DD. Tekshirish qoidasi belgilanishi mumkin foydalanuvchi shablonlarida to enforce the date formatting.

items.title: Yangilash tartibi
items.help_text: This element is used to describe how and with what frequency the sample frame is updated. Masalan: “The lists and boundaries of enumeration areas are updated every ten years at the occasion of the population census cartography work. Listing of households in enumeration areas are updated as and when needed, based on their selection in survey samples.”

items.title: Ma'lumot yig'ish sanalari

props.title: Start
props.help_text: Date the data collection started (for the specified cycle, if any). Enter the date preferably in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM or YYYY). Tekshirish qoidasi belgilanishi mumkin foydalanuvchi shablonlarida to enforce a specific date format.

props.title: End
props.help_text: Date the data collection ended (for the specified cycle, if any). Enter the date preferably in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM or YYYY). Tekshirish qoidasi belgilanishi mumkin foydalanuvchi shablonlarida to enforce a specific date format.

props.title: Cycle
props.help_text: Identification of the cycle of data collection. The cycle attribute permits specification of the relevant cycle, wave, or round of data. Masalan, a household consumption survey could visit households in four phases (one per quarter). Each quarter would be a cycle, and the specific dates of data collection for each quarter would be entered.

items.help_text: Contains the date(s) when the data were collected, which may be different from the dates the data refer to (see "Time periods" element in section "Geographic and time coverage"). Masalan, data may be collected over a period of two weeks ("Dates of data collection") about household expenditures during a reference week ("Time period") preceding the beginning of data collection.

items.title: Vaqt usuli
items.help_text: The time method or time dimension of the data collection. A controlled vocabulary can be used. The entries for this element may include “panel survey”, “cross-section”, “trend study”, or “time-series”.

items.title: Ma'lumot yig'uvchilar
items.help_text: The entity (individual, agency, or institution) responsible for administering the questionnaire or interview or compiling the data.

props.title: Nomi
props.help_text: In most cases, we will record here the name of the agency, not the name of interviewers. Only in the case of very small-scale surveys, with a very limited number of interviewers, the name of persons will be included as well.

props.title: Tashkilot
props.help_text: Tashkilot the data collector "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

props.title: Qisqartma
props.help_text: The abbreviation given to the agency "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

props.title: Rol
props.help_text: O'ziga xos roli the person or agency "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

items.title: Yig'uvchilarni o'qitish
items.help_text: Describes the training provided to data collectors including interviewer training, process testing, compliance with standards etc. This set of elements is repeatable, to capture different aspects of the training process.

props.title: Turi
props.help_text: The type of training being described. Masalan, “Training of interviewers”, “Training of controllers”, “Training of cartographers”, “Training on the use of tablets for data collection”, etc.

props.title: O'qitish
props.help_text: A brief description of the training. This may include information on the dates and duration, audience, location, content, trainers, issues, etc.

items.title: Chastota
items.help_text: For data collected at more than one point in time, the frequency with which the data were collected.

items.title: Ma'lumot yig'ish usuli
items.help_text:
The mode of data collection is the manner in which the interview was conducted or information was gathered. Ideally, a controlled vocabulary will be used to constrain the entries in this field (which could include items like “telephone interview”, “face-to-face paper and pen interview”, “face-to-face computer-assisted interviews (CAPI)”, “mail questionnaire”, “computer-aided telephone interviews (CATI)”, “self-administered web forms”, “measurement by sensor”, and others.

This is a repeatable field, as some data collection activities implement multi-mode data collection (Masalan, a population census can offer respondents the options to submit information via web forms, telephone interviews, mailed forms, or face-to-face interviews.

enum.label: Yuzma-yuz qog'oz va qalam bilan suhbat

items.title: So'rovnomalar
items.help_text:
The questionnaire (or "form", or "research instrument ") used for collecting the data. The following should be mentioned:
- List of questionnaires and short description of each (all questionnaires must be provided as external resources)
- In what language(s) was/were the questionnaire(s) available?
- Information on the questionnaire design process (based on a previous questionnaire, based on a standard model questionnaire, review by stakeholders). If a document was compiled that contains the comments provided by the stakeholders on the draft questionnaire, or a report prepared on the questionnaire testing, a reference to these documents taqdim etilishi mumkin here.

items.title: Vosita ishlab chiqish
items.help_text: Describe any development work on the data collection instrument. This may include a description of the review process, standards followed, and a list of agencies/people consulted.

items.title: Vosita ishlab chiqish turi
items.help_text: The instrument development type. This element will be used when a pre-defined list of options (controlled vocabulary) is available.

items.title: Ma'lumot yig'ish manbalari

props.title: Nomi
props.help_text: The name and other information on the source. Masalan, “United States Internal Revenue Service Quarterly Payroll File”

props.title: Origin
props.help_text: For historical materials, information about the origin(s) of the sources and the rules followed in establishing the sources should be specified. This may not be relevant to survey data.

props.title: Characteristics
props.help_text: Assessment of characteristics and quality of source material. This may not be relevant to survey data.

items.help_text: A description of sources used for developing the methodology of the data collection.

items.title: Ma'lumot yig'ish holati
items.help_text: A description of noteworthy aspects of the data collection situation. Includes information on factors such as cooperativeness of respondents, duration of interviews, number of call-backs, etc.

items.title: Ma'lumotlar yo'qotilishini kamaytirish
items.help_text: A summary of actions taken to minimize data loss. This includes information on actions such as follow-up visits, supervisory checks, historical matching, estimation, etc. Note that this element does not have to include detailed information on response rates, as a specific metadata element is provided for that purpose in section analysis_info / response_rate.

items.title: Nazorat operatsiyalari
items.help_text: This element contains information on the oversight of the data collection, i.e. on methods implemented to facilitate data control usually performed by the primary investigator.

items.title: Ma'lumot yig'ish bo'yicha eslatmalar
items.help_text:
This element is provided to document any specific situations, observations, or events that occurred during data collection. Consider stating such items like: 
    - Was a training of enumerators held? (elaborate) 
    - Was a pilot survey conducted? 
    - Did any events have a bearing on the data quality? (elaborate) 
    - How long did an interview take on average? 
    - In what language(s) were the interviews conducted? 
    - Were there any corrective actions taken by management when problems occurred in the field?

items.title: Metodologiya bo'yicha eslatmalar
items.help_text: This element is provided to capture any additional relevant information on the data collection methodology, which could not fit in the previous metadata elements.

items.title: Data processing
items.help_text: This element is used to describe how data were electronically captured (e.g., entered in the field, in a centralized manner by data entry clerks, captured electronically using tablets and a CAPI application, via web forms, etc.). Information on devices and software used for data capture can also be provided here. Other data processing procedures not captured elsewhere in the documentation can be described here (tabulation, etc.)

props.title: Turi
props.help_text: The type attribute supports better classification of this activity, including the optional use of a controlled vocabulary. The vocabulary could include options like “data capture”, “data validation”, “variable derivation”, “tabulation”, “data visualizations”, anonymization“, ”documentation", etc.

props.title: Description
props.help_text: A description of a data processing task.

items.title: Tozalash operatsiyalari
items.help_text: A description of the methods used to clean or edit the data, e.g., consistency checking, wild code checking, etc. The data editing should contain information on how the data was treated or controlled for in terms of consistency and coherence. This item does not concern the data entry phase but only the editing of data whether manual or automatic. It should provide answers to questions like: Was a hot-deck or a cold-deck technique used to edit the data? Were corrections made automatically (by program), or by visual control of the questionnaire? What software was used? If materials are available (specifications for data editing, report on data editing, programs used for data editing), they should be listed here and provided as external resources in data catalogs (the best documentation of data editing consists of well-documented reproducible scripts).

items.title: Kodlash ko'rsatmalari
items.help_text: The "Coding instructions" elements can be used to describe specific coding instructions used in data processing, cleaning, or tabulation. Providing this information may however be complex and very tedious for datasets with a significant number of variables, where hundreds of commands are used to process the data. An alternative option, preferable in many cases, will be to publish reproducible data editing, tabulation and analysis scripts together with the data, as related resources.

props.title: Turi
props.help_text: The “type” attribute supports the classification of this activity (e.g. “topcoding”). A controlled vocabulary can be used.

props.title: Tegishli jarayonlar
props.help_text: The "Related processes" links a coding instruction to one or more processes such as “data editing”, “recoding”, “imputations and derivations”, “tabulation”, etc.

props.title: Description
props.help_text: A description of the code/command, in a human readable form.

props.title: Buyruq
props.help_text: The command code for the coding instruction.

props.title: Rasmiy til
props.help_text: The language of the command code, e.g. “Stata”, “R”, “SPSS”, “SAS”, “Python”, etc.

items.title: Faoliyatlar
items.help_text: Describe the process of study development as a series of development activities. These activities can be typed using a controlled vocabulary. Describe the activity, listing participants with their role and affiliation, resources used (sources of information), and the outcome of the development activity.

items.title: Tadqiqot faoliyatlari
items.help_text: This section is used to describe the process that led to the production of the final output of the study, from its inception/design to the dissemination of the final output. Each activity is documented separately. The Generic Statistical Business Process Model (GSBPM) provides a useful decomposition of such a process, which can be used to list the activities to be described.

props.title: Turi
props.help_text: The type of activity. A controlled vocabulary can be used, possibly comprising the main components of the GSBPM: {Needs specification, Design, Build, Collect, Process, Analyze, Disseminate, Evaluate}).

props.title: Description
props.help_text: A brief description of the activity.

props.title: Ishtirokchilar
Nomi
props.help_text: Name of the participating person or organization.

props.title: Tashkilot
props.help_text: Affiliation of shaxs yoki tashkilot "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

props.title: Rol
props.help_text: Specific role (participation) of shaxs yoki tashkilot "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.
A list of participants (persons or organizations) in the activity. This is a repeatable set of elements; each participant can be documented separately.

props.title: Manbalar
Nomi
props.help_text: The name of the resource.

props.title: Origin
props.help_text: The origin of the resource "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

props.title: Characteristics
props.help_text: The characteristics of the resource "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.
A description of the data sources and other resources used to implement the activity.

props.title: Faoliyat natijasi
props.help_text: A brief description of the main outcome of the activity.

items.title: Standartlarga muvofiqlik
items.help_text: A statement on compliance with standard quality assessment procedures. The list of these standards can be documented in the element "Standards".

items.title: Sifat standartlari
items.help_text: An itemized list of quality standards complied with during the execution of the study.

props.title: Standart
props.help_text: The name of the quality standard, if such a standard was used. Include the date when the standard was published, and the version of the standard with which the study is compliant, and possibly a URL.

props.title: Ishlab chiqaruvchi
props.help_text: The producer of the quality standard mentioned in "Standard".

items.title: Boshqa sifat bayonoti
items.help_text: Any additional statement on the quality of the data, entered as free text. This can be independent of any particular quality standard.

items.title: Tugallanish sanasi
items.help_text: Ex-post evaluations are frequently done within large statistical or research organizations, in particular when a study is intended to be repeated. Such evaluations are recommended by the Generic Statistical Business Process Model (GSBPM). This section of the schema is used to describe the evaluation procedures and their outcomes. This field is used to capture the date the ex-post evaluation was completed. It is recommended to enter the date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM). Tekshirish qoidasi belgilanishi mumkin foydalanuvchi shablonlarida sana formatini majburlash uchun.

items.title: Baholash turi
items.help_text: The "Type" attribute identifies the type of ex-post evaluation, with or without the use of a controlled vocabulary.

items.title: Baholovchilar
items.help_text: The "Evaluator" element identifies the person(s) and/or organization(s) involved in the ex-post evaluation.

props.title: Nomi
props.help_text: The name of shaxs yoki tashkilot involved in the evaluation.

props.title: Qisqartma
props.help_text: An abbreviation for the organization "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

props.title: Tashkilot
props.help_text: Tashkilot the individual or organization "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

props.title: Rol
props.help_text: The specific role played by the individual or organization "Nomi" da qayd etilgan in the evaluation process.

items.title: Baholash jarayoni
items.help_text: A description of the ex-post evaluation process. This may include information on the period when the evaluation was conducted, cost/budget, relevance, institutional or legal arrangements, etc.

items.title: Baholash natijalari
items.help_text: A description of the outcomes of the ex-post evaluation. It may include a reference to an evaluation report.

items.title: Javob berish darajalari
items.help_text: The response rate is the percentage of sample units that participated in the survey based on the original sample size. Omissions may occur due to refusal to participate, impossibility to locate the respondent, or other reason. This element is used to provide a narrative description of the response rate, possibly by stratum or other criteria, and if possible with an identification of possible causes. If information is available on the causes of non-response (refusal/not found/other), it can be reported here. This field can also be used to describe non-responses in population censuses.

items.title: Tanlanma xatolari
items.help_text: Sampling errors are intended to measure how precisely one can estimate a population value from a given sample. For sampling surveys, yaxshi amaliyot to calculate and publish sampling error. This field is used to provide information on these calculations (not to provide the sampling errors themselves, which should be made available in publications or reports). Information taqdim etilishi mumkin on which ratios/indicators have been subjected to the calculation of sampling errors, and on the software used for computing the sampling error. Reference to a report or other document where the results can be found can also be provided.

items.title: Ma'lumotlarning boshqa baholari
items.help_text: This section is used to report any other action taken to assess the reliability of the data, or any observations regarding data quality. Describe here issues such as response variance, interviewer and response bias, question bias, etc. For a population census, this can include information on the main results of a post enumeration survey (a report should be provided in external resources and mentioned here); it can also include relevant comparisons with data from other sources that can be used as benchmarks.

items.title: Ma'lumotlar to'plamining mavjudligi
items.help_text: Information on availability and storage of the collection

items.title: Location
items.help_text: Name of the location where the data collection is currently stored.

items.title: Joylashuv URL
items.help_text: The URL of the website of the location where the data collection is currently stored.

items.title: Asl arxiv
items.help_text: Archive from which the data collection was obtained, if any (the originating archive). Note that the schema we propose provides an element provenance, which is not part of the DDI, that can be used to document the origin of a dataset.

items.title: Mavjudlik holati
items.help_text: A statement of the data availability. An archive may need to indicate that a collection is unavailable because it is embargoed for a period of time, because it has been superseded, because a new edition is imminent, etc. This element will rarely be used.

items.title: To'plam hajmi
items.help_text: Extent of the collection. This is a summary of the number of physical files that exist in a collection. We will record here the number of files that contain data and note whether the collection contains other machine-readable documentation and/or other supplementary files and information such as data dictionaries, data definition statements, or data collection instruments. This element will rarely be used.

items.title: To'liqlik
items.help_text: This item indicates the relationship of the data collected to the amount of data coded and stored in the data collection. Information as to why certain items of collected information were not included in the data file stored by the archive should be provided here. Example: “Because of embargo provisions, data values for some variables have been masked. Users should consult the data definition statements to see which variables are under embargo.” This element will rarely be used.

items.title: Fayllar soni
items.help_text: The total number of physical files associated with a collection. This element will rarely be used.

items.title: Notes
items.help_text: Additional information on the dataset availability, not included in one of the elements above.

items.title: Ma'lumotlarga kirish va foydalanish
items.help_text: Information on terms of use for the data collection

items.title: Maxfiylik deklaratsiyasi
items.help_text: This element is used to determine if signing of a confidentiality declaration is needed to access a resource. We may indicate here what Affidavit of Confidentiality must be signed before the data can be accessed. Another option is to include this information in the element "Access conditions". If there is no confidentiality issue, this field can be left blank.

props.title: Required
props.help_text: Indicate if a confidentiality declaration is required to access a resource. The "Required" element is used to aid machine processing of this element.

props.title: Text
props.help_text: A statement on confidentiality and limitations to data use. This statement does not replace a more comprehensive data agreement (see "Access condition").

props.title: Form ID
props.help_text: Indicates the number or ID of the confidentiality declaration form that the user must fill out.

props.title: Form URL
props.help_text: The "Form URL" element is used to provide a link to an online confidentiality declaration form.

items.title: Maxsus ruxsatlar
items.help_text: This element is used to determine if any special permission is required to access a resource.

props.title: Required
props.help_text: Indicate if special permissions are required to access a resource. The "Required" element is used to aid machine processing of this element.

props.title: Text
props.help_text: A statement on the special permissions required to access the dataset.

props.title: Form URL
props.help_text: The "Form URL" is used to provide a link to a special on-line permission form.

props.title: Form ID
props.help_text: The “Form ID” indicates the number or ID of the special permissions form that the user must fill out.

items.title: Cheklovlar
items.help_text: Any restrictions on access to or use of the collection such as privacy certification or distribution restrictions should be indicated here. These can be restrictions applied by the author, producer, or distributor of the data. This element can Masalan contain a statement (extracted from the DDI documentation) like: “In preparing the data file(s) for this collection, the National Center for Health Statistics (NCHS) has removed direct identifiers and characteristics that might lead to identification of data subjects. As an additional precaution NCHS requires, under Section 308(d) of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 242m), that data collected by NCHS not be used for any purpose other than statistical analysis and reporting. NCHS further requires that analysts not use the data to learn the identity of any persons or establishments and that the director of NCHS be notified if any identities are inadvertently discovered. Users ordering data are expected to adhere to these restrictions.”

items.title: Aloqa ma'lumotlari

props.title: Nomi
props.help_text: Name of the person. Note that in some cases, it might be better to provide a title/function than the actual name of the person. Keep in mind that people do not stay forever in their position.

props.title: Tashkilot
props.help_text: Affiliation of the person "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

props.title: URL
props.help_text: URL for the person; it can be the URL of the organization the person belongs to.

props.title: Elektron pochta
props.help_text: The email element is used to indicate an email address for the contact individual mentioned in name. Ideally, a generic email address should be provided. It is easy to configure a mail server in such a way that all messages sent to the generic email address would be automatically forwarded to some staff members.

items.help_text: Users of the data may need further clarification and information on the terms of use and conditions to access the data. This set of elements is used to identify the contact persons who can be used as resource persons regarding problems or questions raised by the user community.

items.title: Iqtibos talabi
items.help_text: A citation requirement that indicates the way that the dataset should be referenced when cited in any publication. Providing a citation requirement will guarantee that the data producer gets proper credit, and that results of analysis can be linked to the proper version of the dataset. The data access policy should explicitly mention the obligation to comply with the citation requirement. The citation should include at least the primary investigator, the name and abbreviation of the dataset, the reference year, and the version number. Include also a website where the data or information on the data is made available by the official data depositor. Ideally, the citation requirement will include a DOI (see the DataCite website for recommendations).

items.title: Depozit talabi
items.help_text: Information regarding data users’ responsibility for informing archives of their use of data through providing citations to the published work or providing copies of the manuscripts.

items.title: Kirish shartlari
items.help_text: Indicates any additional information that will assist the user in understanding the access and use conditions of the data collection. Note that if this information is subject to change, it should not be captured in the study metadata but provided in a data catalog page.

items.title: Javobgarlikdan voz kechish
items.help_text: A disclaimer limits the liability that the data producer or data custodian has regarding the use of the data. A standard legal statement should be used for all datasets from a same agency. The following formulation could be used: The user of the data acknowledges that the original collector of the data, the authorized distributor of the data, and the relevant funding agency bear no responsibility for use of the data or for interpretations or inferences based upon such uses.

items.title: Notes
items.help_text: Any additional information related to data access that is not contained in the specific metadata elements provided in the section "Data access and use".

items.title: Bibliographic citation
items.help_text: Complete bibliographic reference containing all of the standard elements of a citation that can be used to cite the work. The `'bib_citation_format'` field is provided to enable specification of the particular citation style used, e.g., APA, MLA, Chicago, etc.

items.title: Bibliografik iqtibos
items.help_text: Complete bibliographic reference containing all of the standard elements of a citation that can be used to cite the study. A "Citation format" element is also provided to enable specification of the particular citation style used, Masalan APA, MLA, or Chicago.

items.title: Iqtibos formati
items.help_text: This element is used to specify the particular citation style used in the field "Bibliographic citation", Masalan "APA", "MLA", or "Chicago".

items.title: Holdings information
items.help_text: Information concerning either the physical or electronic holdings of the cited work. Attributes include: location--The physical location where a copy is held; callno--The call number for a work at the location specified; and URI--A URN or URL for accessing the electronic copy of the cited work.

items.title: Fond haqida ma'lumot
items.help_text: Information concerning either the physical or electronic holdings of the study being described. Note that this information should only be provided when the dataset is stored in a location that is unlikely to change. If a dataset is stored in multiple and/or changing locations, this information can be left empty as it would in many cases be inaccurate.

props.title: Nomi
props.help_text: Name of the physical or electronic holdings of the cited study.

props.title: Location
props.help_text: The physical location where a copy of the study is held.

props.title: Call number
props.help_text: The call number at the location specified in "Location".

props.title: URL
props.help_text: A URL for accessing the electronic copy of the cited study from the location "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

items.title: Tarqatuvchilar
items.help_text: The organization(s) designated by the author or producer to generate copies of the study output including any necessary editions or revisions.

props.title: Nomi
props.help_text: The name of the distributor. It can be an individual or an organization.

props.title: Qisqartma
props.help_text: The official abbreviation of the organization "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

props.title: Tashkilot
props.help_text: "Nomi" da qayd etilgan shaxs yoki tashkilotning tashkilot.

props.title: URL
props.help_text: A link (URL) to the ordering service or download facility website.

items.title: Contacts

props.title: Nomi
props.help_text: The name of shaxs yoki tashkilot that can be contacted.

props.title: Tashkilot
props.help_text: "Nomi" da qayd etilgan shaxs yoki tashkilotning tashkilot.

props.title: Email
props.help_text: An email address for the contact "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

props.title: URL
props.help_text: A link (URL) to the contact "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

items.help_text: Names and addresses of individuals responsible for the study. Individuals listed as contact persons will be used as resource persons regarding problems or questions raised by users.

items.title: Depozitchi

props.title: Nomi
props.help_text: The name of the depositor. It can be an individual or an organization.

props.title: Qisqartma
props.help_text: The official abbreviation of the organization "Nomi" da qayd etilgan.

props.title: Tashkilot
props.help_text: "Nomi" da qayd etilgan shaxs yoki tashkilotning tashkilot.

props.title: URL
props.help_text: A link (URL) to the depositor website.

items.help_text: The name of the person or institution who provided this study to the archive storing it.

items.title: Depozit sanasi
items.help_text: The date that the study was deposited with the archive that originally received it. The date should be entered in the ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM or YYYY). The exact date should be provided when possible. A regular expression can be entered in customized user templates to validate the content of this element.

items.title: Tarqatish sanasi
items.help_text: Date that the study was made available for distribution. The date should be entered in the ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM or YYYY). The exact date should be provided when possible. A regular expression can be entered in customized user templates to validate the content of this element.
This section contains information about the data collection's scope across several dimensions, including substantive content, geography, and time.
items.title: Boshqa ma'lumotlar
Byudjet
items.help_text: The budget of a study will ideally be described by budget line. The currency used to describe the budget should be specified. This element can also be used to document issues related to the budget (e.g., documenting possible under-run and over-run).

items.title: Tadqiqot sinfi
items.help_text: This element can be used to give the data archive’s class or study status number, which indicates the processing status of the study. But it can also be used as an element to indicate the type of study, based on a controlled vocabulary. The element is repeatable, allowing one study to belong to more than one class.

items.title: Tadqiqot bo'yicha eslatmalar
items.help_text: This element can be used to provide additional information on the study which cannot be accommodated in the specific metadata elements of the schema, in the form of a free text field.

items.title: File ID
items.help_text: A unique file identifier (within the metadata document, not necessarily within a catalog). This will typically be the electronic file name.

items.title: Fayl nomi
items.help_text: This is not the name of the electronic file (which is provided in the previous element). It is a short title (label) that will help distinguish a particular file/part from other files/parts in the dataset.

items.title: Fayl turi
items.help_text: The type of data files. Masalan, raw data (ASCII), or software-dependent files such as SAS / Stata / SPSS data file, etc. Provide specific information (e.g. Stata 10 or Stata 15, SPSS Windows or SPSS Export, etc.) Note that in an on-line catalog, data can be made available in multiple formats. In such case, the file_type element is not useful.

items.title: Description
items.help_text: The "File ID" and "File name" elements provide limited information on the content of the file. The description element is used to provide a more detailed description of the file content. This description should clearly distinguish collected variables and derived variables. It is also useful to indicate the availability in the data file of some particular variables such as the weighting coefficients. If the file contains derived variables, yaxshi amaliyot to refer to the computer program that generated it. Information about the data file(s) that comprises a collection.

items.title: Holatlar soni
items.help_text: Number of cases or observations in the data file. The value is 0 by default. This information will be automatically generated in Metadata Editors that provide an option to import data files.

items.title: O'zgaruvchilar soni
items.help_text: Number of variables in the data file. The value is 0 by default.  This information will be automatically generated in Metadata Editors that provide an option to import data files.

items.title: Producer
items.help_text: The name of the agency that produced the data file. Most data files will have been produced by the survey primary investigator. In some cases however, auxiliary or derived files from other producers may be released with a data set. This may Masalan be a file containing derived variables generated by a researcher.

items.title: Ma'lumot tekshiruvlari
items.help_text: Use this element if needed to provide information about the types of checks and operations that have been performed on the data file to make sure that the data are as correct as possible, e.g. consistency checking, wildcode checking, etc. Note that the information included here should be specific to the data file. Information about data processing checks that have been carried out on the data collection (study) as a whole should be provided in the Data editing element at the study level. You may also provide here a reference to an external resource that contains the specifications for the data processing checks (that same information may be provided also in the Data Editing filed in the Tadqiqot tavsifi section).

items.title: Etishmayotgan ma'lumotlar
items.help_text: A description of missing data (number of missing cases, cause of missing values, etc.)

items.title: Versiya
items.help_text: The version of the data file. A data file may undergo various changes and modifications. File specific versions can be tracked in this element. This field will in most cases be left empty.

items.title: Ma'lumot fayli bo'yicha eslatmalar
items.help_text: This field aims to provide information on the specific data file not covered elsewhere.

items.title: File ID
items.help_text: A dataset can be composed of multiple data files. The File ID is the name of the data file that contains the variable being documented. This file name should correspond to a File ID listed in the "Data file description" section of the DDI. When using a Metadata Editor application, this information will typically be automatically generated when importing data files. This information is required.

items.title: O'zgaruvchi ID
items.help_text: Noyob identifikator given to the variable (unique within the full dataset, not only within a data file). This information is required. This is NOT the variable name (captured in element "Variable name"). This can be a system-generated ID, such as a sequential number within each data file. When using a Metadata Editor application, the ID will typically be automatically generated when importing data files.

items.title: O'zgaruvchi nomi
items.help_text: The name of the variable in the data file. This information is required. The name should be entered exactly as found in the data file (not abbreviated or converted to upper or lower cases, as some software applications are case-sensitive). This information can be programmatically extracted from the data file. When using a Metadata Editor application, the variable name will typically be extracted from the imported data files. The variable name is limited to eight characters in some statistical analysis software such as SAS or SPSS. A variable name must be unique within a data file (not within a full dataset, as a same variable may appear in multiple data files).

items.title: O'zgaruvchi yorlig'i
items.help_text: All variables should have a label that provides a short but clear indication of what the variable contains. Ideally, all variables in a data file will have a unique label. File formats like Stata or SPSS often contain variable labels. When using a Metadata Editor application, the variable name will typically be extracted from the imported data files. Variable labels can also be found in data dictionaries in software applications like Survey Solutions or CsPro. Avoid using the question itself as a label (specific elements are available to capture the literal question text). Think of a label as what you would want to see in a tabulation of the variables. Keep in mind that software applications like Stata and others impose a limit to the number of characters in a label (often, 80).

items.title: Universe
items.help_text: The universe at the variable level defines the population the question applied to. It reflects skip patterns in a questionnaire. This information can typically be copy/pasted from the survey questionnaire. Try to be as specific as possible. This information is critical for the analyst, as it explains why missing values may be found in a variable.

items.title: O'zgaruvchi ta'rifi
items.help_text: This element provides a space to describe the variable in detail. Not all variables require a definition.

items.title: Kontseptsiyalar
items.help_text: The general subject to which the parent element may be seen as pertaining. This element serves the same purpose as the keywords and topic classification elements, but at the variable description level.

props.title: Sarlavha
props.help_text: The name (label) of the concept.

props.title: Vocabulary
props.help_text: The controlled vocabulary, if any, from which the concept `title’ was taken.

props.title: URL
props.help_text: A link (URL) to the controlled vocabulary mentioned in "Vocabulary".

items.title: Xulosa statistikalari
items.help_text: The DDI metadata standard provides multiple elements to capture various summary statistics such as minimum, maximum, or mean values (weighted and un-weighted) for each variable. The content of the "Summary statistics" section will be easy to fill out programmatically (using R or Python) or using a specialized DDI metadata editor, which can read the data file and generate the summary statistics.

props.title: Turi
props.help_text: The type of statistics being shown: mean, median, mode, valid cases, invalid cases, minimum, maximum, or standard deviation.

props.title: Value
props.help_text: The value of the summary statistics mentioned in type.

props.title: Weighted
props.help_text: Indicates whether the statistics reported in value are weighted or not (for variables in sample surveys). Enter “weighted” if weighted, otherwise leave this element empty.

items.title: O'zgaruvchi toifalari

props.title: Code
props.help_text: The code of the category.

props.title: Label
props.help_text: The label of the category (or "value label")

props.title: Turi
props.help_text: Type of statistics, e.g. "Count".

props.title: Value
props.help_text: Estimate

props.title: Weighted
props.help_text: Indicates whether the "Value" is weighted (for sample surveys) or not.

items.help_text: A description of the categories (codes with labels) that apply to the variable, for categorical variables. The element also includes summary statistics at the category level.

items.title: Standart toifalar
items.help_text: This element is used to indicate that the codes used for a categorical variable are from a standard international or other classification, like COICOP, ISIC, ISO country codes, etc.

props.title: Nomi
props.help_text: The name of the classification, e.g. “International Standard Industrial Classification of All Economic Activities (ISIC), Revision 4”

props.title: Source
props.help_text: The source of the classification, Masalan “United Nations”.

props.title: Date
props.help_text: The version (typically a date) of the classification used for the study.

props.title: URL
props.help_text: A URL to a website where an electronic copy and more information on the classification can be obtained.

items.title: Interval turi
items.help_text: This element indicates whether the intervals between values for the variable are discrete or continuous.

items.title: Kasr nuqtalari
items.help_text: This element refers to the number of decimal points in the values of the variable. It must be a numeric value.

items.title: Kalit o'zgaruvchi
items.help_text: This element indicates whether the variable is a key variable (value "1") or not (value "0"). Key variables are used to identify records in a data file. They are typically unique identifiers such as ID numbers, or other variables that can be used to uniquely identify a record.

items.title: Vazn koeffitsienti
items.help_text: This element, which applies to dataset from sample surveys, indicates whether the variable is a sample weight (value “1”) or not (value "0). Sample weights play an important role in the calculation of summary statistics and sampling errors, and should therefore be flagged.

items.title: Xavfsizlik
items.help_text: This element is used to provide information regarding levels of access, e.g., public, subscriber, need to know.

items.title: O'zgaruvchi bo'yicha eslatmalar
items.help_text: This element is provided to record any additional or auxiliary information related to the specific variable.

items.title: Respondent
items.help_text: Provides information regarding who provided the information contained within the variable, e.g., head of household, respondent, proxy, interviewer.

items.title: Savol oldi matni
items.help_text: The pre-question texts are the instructions provided to the interviewers and printed in the questionnaire before the literal question. This does not apply to all variables. Do not confuse this with instructions provided in the interviewer’s manual.

items.title: To'g'ridan-to'g'ri savol
items.help_text: The literal question is the full text of the questionnaire as the enumerator is expected to ask it when conducting the interview. This does not apply to all variables (it does not apply to derived variables).

items.title: Savol keyingi matni
items.help_text: The post-question texts are instructions provided to the interviewers, printed in the questionnaire after the literal question. Post-question can be used to enter information on skips provided in the questionnaire. This does not apply to all variables. Do not confuse this with instructions provided in the interviewer’s manual. With the previous two elements, one should be able to understand how the question was formulated in a questionnaire.

items.title: Oldinga o'tish
items.help_text: Contains a reference to the IDs of possible following questions. This can be used to document forward skip instructions.

items.title: Orqaga o'tish
items.help_text: Contains a reference to IDs of possible preceding questions. This can be used to document backward skip instructions.

items.title: Suhbat o'tkazuvchi ko'rsatmalari
items.help_text: Specific instructions to the individual conducting an interview. The content will typically be entered by copy/pasting instructions in the interviewer’s manual (or in the CAPI application). In cases where the same instructions relate to multiple variables, repeat the same information in the metadata for all these variables.

items.title: Kodlovchi ko'rsatmalari
items.help_text: The coder instructions for the variable. These are any special instructions to those who converted information from one form to another (e.g., textual to numeric) for a particular variable.

items.title: Imolyatsiya
items.help_text: Imputation is the process of estimating values for variables when a value is missing. The element is used to describe the procedure used to impute values when missing.

items.title: Hosila
items.help_text: Used only in the case of a derived variable, this element provides both a description of how the derivation was performed and the command used to generate the derived variable, as well as a specification of the other variables in the study used to generate the derivation. The Derivation element is used to provide a brief description of this process. As full transparency in derivation processes is critical to build trust and ensure replicability or reproducibility, the information captured in this element will often not be sufficient. A reference to a document and/or computer program can in such case be provided in this element, and the document/scripts provided as external resources.

items.title: Turi
items.help_text: Indicates if the variable is numeric, fixed string, dynamic string, or date. Numeric variables are used to store any number, integer or floating point (decimals). A fixed string variable has a predefined length which enables the publisher to handle this data type more efficiently. Dynamic string variables can be used to store open-ended questions.

items.title: Nomi
items.help_text: The name of the particular, proprietary format used.

items.title: Eslatma
items.help_text: Additional information on the variable format.

items.title: Boshlang'ich pozitsiya
items.help_text: The starting position of the variable when the data are saved in an ASCII fixed-format data file.

items.title: Yakuniy pozitsiya
items.help_text: The end position of the variable when the data are saved in an ASCII fixed-format data file.

items.title: Kenglik
items.help_text: The length of the variable (the maximum number of characters used for its values) in an ASCII fixed-format data file.

items.title: Yozuv segmenti raqami
items.help_text: Record segment number, deck or card number the variable is located on.

items.title: Guruh ID
items.help_text: In a dataset, variables are grouped by data file. For the convenience of users, the DDI allows data curators to organize the variables into different, "virtual" groups to organize variables by theme, type of respondent, or any other criteria. Grouping variables is optional, and will not impact the way variables are stored in the data files. One variable can belong to more than a group, and a group of variables can contain variables from more than one data file. The variable groups do not necessarily have to cover all variables in the data files. Variable groups can also contain other variable groups. Group ID is Noyob identifikator (unique within the DDI metadata file) for the variable group.

items.title: O'zgaruvchilar
items.help_text: The list of variables ("Variable ID") in the group. Enter a list with items separated by a space, e.g. "V21 V22 V30".

items.title: O'zgaruvchi guruhlari
items.help_text: The variable groups ("Group ID") that are embedded in this variable group. Enter a list with items separated by a space, e.g. "VG2 VG5".

items.title: Guruh turi
items.help_text: The type of grouping of the variables. A controlled vocabulary should be used. The DDI proposes the following vocabulary: "section", "multipleResp", "grid", "display", "repetition", "subject", "version", "iteration", "analysis", "pragmatic", "record", "file", "randomized", "other". A description of the groups can be found in a document available at https://zenodo.org/record/3823051/files/maddiewshop.pdf, by W. Thomas, W. Block, R. Wozniak and J. Buysse.

items.title: Label
items.help_text: A short description of the variable group.

items.title: Description
items.help_text: A more detailed description of variable group than the one provided in "Label".

items.title: Universe
items.help_text: The universe can be a population of individuals, households, facilities, organizations, or others, which can be defined by any type of criteria (e.g., "adult males", "private schools", "small and medium-size enterprises", etc.)

items.title: Notes
items.help_text: Used to provide additional information about the variable group.

items.title: Asoslash
items.help_text: A brief rationale for the variable grouping.

items.title: Teglar

props.title: Teg
props.help_text: A user-defined tag.

props.title: Teg guruhi
props.help_text: A user-defined group (optional) to which the tag belongs. Grouping tags allows implementation of controlled facets in data catalogs.

items.help_text: Tags, especially when organized in tag groups, provide a powerful and flexible solution to enable custom facets (filters) in data catalogs. Note that tags and tag groups are not element from the DDI Codebook standard.
